calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Called with case numbers 205402 and 288207. Anne Marie Paolini-Mori appearing for and with co-executors in William Naughton estate and for petitioner in the Helen Naughton estate. Stephen Gould appearing for and with Debra Dolch. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Ms. Paolini-Mori agrees to suspension of the co-executors' powers over personal property and appointing Debra Dolch as special administrator for both estates to handle the German claims. This matter is continued to 8/16 for status. Court wants a status report from the co-executors on the eschated property and real property. Order appointing special administrator signed. Letters will expire until further order of court. Court will issue order consolidating 288206 with 205402. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. ADA Reporter: Patty Hubble. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-15-2006 Continued To Jun-19-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Case called with case no. 205402 and case no. 288207. Stephen Gould appearing for Debra Dolch. Anne Marie Paolini-Mori appearing for and with co-executors William Naughton and Peter Naughton. Elizabeth Moore, Peter Naughton's daughter is also in court. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Peter Naughton is a contractor and he will bring the property to code. Court is consolidating the two William Naughton cases into the lower number 205402. Ms. Dolch to remain as the Special Administator in both the William and Helen estates to continue to pursue the claim in Germany. Her appointment will expire until further order of court. Mr. Ferdon states his concerns regarding the non action by William Naughton for a long period. Court is allowing the co-executors to continue to be the personal representatives. Court wants a status report to be filed by 6/12. Status is set for hearing on 6/19. Ms. Paolini-Mori will have to give notice of the status report to the beneficiary in Germany. She informs the court that she may be able to get a waiver from the beneficiary. If the court determines that the co-executors are acting sufficiently, they will be removed and Ms. Dolch will be appointed on 6/19. Mr. Ferdon objects to Ms. Dolch's appointment. The Public Administrator asserts their priority. Ms. Dolch's petition for appointment in both estates is continued to 6/19. Mr. Naughton's petition in Helen's estate set for hearing on 5/30 is also continued to 6/19. Mr. Gould will submit the language that he wishes to include in the order consolidating cases. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722 and Patty Hubble ADA Court Reporter. Clerk: Ella Yip.
